query: Florida 2026 gubernatorial GOP primary debate schedule Byron Donalds James Fishback David Jolly - any debates scheduled or held summer 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary of findings:

**Key answer to your question:** One informal GOP primary debate WAS held in summer 2026 (July 2), but no official party-sanctioned primary debate is scheduled, and the frontrunner refuses to participate.

**Critical correction:** David Jolly is NOT a GOP primary candidate — he's the leading **Democratic** candidate (former GOP congressman who switched parties in April 2025). The GOP primary field is **Byron Donalds, James Fishback, Jay Collins (Lt. Gov.), and Paul Renner (former House Speaker)**.

**What happened / is scheduled:**
- **July 2, 2026:** An unofficial "undercard" GOP debate ("The Future of Florida") was held in Fort Lauderdale, moderated by podcast host Patrick Bet-David and streamed on YouTube. Renner, Collins, and Fishback participated; **Donalds skipped it** (mentioned 60+ times in absentia).
- **Donalds refuses to debate** rivals, saying they lack traction and can't win statewide.
- **July 7, 2026:** The Republican Party of Florida declared it has **not sanctioned any statewide debate** before the Aug. 18 primary — sparking backlash and public criticism from Gov. DeSantis, who said the party lacks authority to block televised primary debates.
- **Tentatively announced (not finalized):** General-election debates at FAU via the "Decision 2026: Before YOU Vote" series — Senate Oct. 13, governor Oct. 15, 2026, with a 12.5% Mason-Dixon polling threshold. RPOF and Jolly called this "premature."

The full report with all sources and data points has been submitted via the structured output.

sources.title: Donalds still leads Florida GOP governor's race, but rivals press him on lawsuit, debates and polling
sources.snippet: Collins sued Fishback over Florida's seven-year residency requirement; debates and polling disputes continue.

key_facts: Florida GOP gubernatorial primary is August 18, 2026; general election November 3, 2026; DeSantis is term-limited.

An unofficial GOP gubernatorial debate was held July 2, 2026 in Fort Lauderdale, moderated by podcast host Patrick Bet-David and streamed on YouTube; participants were Paul Renner, Jay Collins, and James Fishback.
Frontrunner Byron Donalds SKIPPED the July 2 debate and was mentioned ~60+ times; he consistently refuses to debate primary rivals, saying they can't win statewide.
David Jolly is the leading DEMOCRATIC candidate (former GOP congressman who switched to Democrat April 2025), NOT a GOP primary candidate; his running mate is Gwen Graham.
GOP primary field: Byron Donalds (Trump-backed frontrunner), James Fishback, Jay Collins (Lt. Gov.), Paul Renner (former House Speaker).
On July 7, 2026, the Republican Party of Florida stated it has NOT sanctioned any statewide debate before the August primary, drawing backlash and criticism from Gov. DeSantis.

General-election debates tentatively announced by the 'Decision 2026: Before YOU Vote' series at Florida Atlantic University: Senate Oct 13 and governor Oct 15, 2026, but called 'premature' by RPOF and Jolly.
October debate qualification threshold: at least 12.5% support (including margin of error) in a September Mason-Dixon poll.
Jay Collins has sued James Fishback challenging his eligibility over Florida's seven-year residency requirement.
No official/party-sanctioned GOP primary debate is scheduled before the August 18 primary.
